name: Hung Kings
title: Founding Ancestors
description: The first kings of the Vietnamese people, credited with establishing the first early state known as Van Lang.
achievements: Established the Van Lang state
Started 4000 years of civilization
funFact: Legend says there were 18 generations of Hung Kings, each living a very long life and loving their people like children!

name: Saint Giong
hometown: Phu Dong Village, Gia Lam, Hanoi
title: Phu Dong Thien Vuong
description: A boy from Giong village who grew remarkably fast, rode an iron horse, and used bamboo to defeat the An invaders to save the country.
achievements: Defeated the An invaders
Symbol of youth's strength
funFact: After defeating the enemy, Saint Giong rode his horse and flew straight to heaven from the peak of Soc Mountain!

name: An Duong Vuong
title: King of Au Lac
description: The king who unified Lac Viet and Tay Au, built the spiral-shaped Co Loa Citadel, and possessed a magical crossbow.
achievements: Built Co Loa Citadel
Established the Au Lac state
funFact: Co Loa Citadel was built with 9 spiral rings, thousands of meters long, and was an extremely solid fortress!

name: The Trung Sisters
hometown: Me Linh, Vinh Phuc (now part of Hanoi)
position: The first Queens of the nation
title: First Queens
description: Two brave sisters who rode elephants into battle to drive out the Han army, regaining independence for the nation after many years of suffering.
achievements: Successful uprising in 40 AD
Proclaimed Queens, established capital in Me Linh
funFact: The oath of the Trung Sisters still echoes: 'First, I will cleanse the national enemy / Second, I will restore the ancient Hung lineage'!

name: Lady Trieu
position: Leader of the uprising against the Wu army
title: Nhuy Kieu General
description: A brave woman with extraordinary spirit: 'I want to ride the strong wind, tread the fierce waves, and slay the whale in the Eastern Sea'.
achievements: Uprising against the Wu army
Symbol of indomitable will

name: Ngo Quyen
position: Founding King of the Ngo Dynasty
title: Tien Ngo Vuong
description: A talented general who used a wooden stake field on the Bach Dang River to completely end 1000 years of Northern domination.
achievements: Great victory over the Southern Han in 938
Opened an era of long-term independence and autonomy
funFact: Ngo Quyen was the first to abolish the enemy's titles and proclaim himself King, restoring independence to the nation!

name: Dinh Bo Linh
position: Founding Emperor of the Dinh Dynasty
title: Dinh Tien Hoang
description: A buffalo boy who played with reed flags and courageously suppressed the 12 warlords, unifying the country.
achievements: Suppressed the 12 warlords
Named the country Dai Co Viet

name: Le Hoan
position: Founding Emperor of the Early Le Dynasty
title: Le Dai Hanh
description: A brave general who was awarded the imperial robe by the Queen Mother and led the army to defeat the Song invaders.
achievements: Defeated the Song army in 981
Founded the Early Le Dynasty

name: Ly Thai To
position: Founding Emperor of the Ly Dynasty
title: The one who moved the capital to Thang Long
description: A benevolent king who moved the capital from Hoa Lu to Thang Long, opening a new era of prosperity.
achievements: Moved the capital to Thang Long in 1010
Founded the powerful Ly Dynasty

name: Ly Thuong Kiet
position: Grand Commander of the Ly Dynasty
title: Author of the first divine poem
description: An outstanding general who wrote the poem 'Nam Quoc Son Ha' to affirm national sovereignty on the Nhu Nguyet River.
achievements: Defeated the Song army on the Nhu Nguyet River
Author of the poem 'Nam Quoc Son Ha'

name: Tran Hung Dao
position: Supreme Commander of the Tran Dynasty
title: Hung Dao Dai Vuong
description: An outstanding military genius who led the people of the Tran Dynasty to defeat the powerful Mongol invaders three times.
achievements: Great victories over the Mongol army
Wrote 'Hich Tuong Si' to inspire the troops

name: Le Loi
position: Founding Emperor of the Later Le Dynasty
title: Le Thai To
description: The leader of the arduous 10-year Lam Son uprising to drive out the Ming invaders.
achievements: Drove out the Ming invaders
Founded the Later Le (Early Le) Dynasty

name: Nguyen Trai
title: World Cultural Figure
description: A talented advisor to King Le Loi, author of 'Binh Ngo Dai Cao' affirming the nation's independence.
achievements: Wrote 'Binh Ngo Dai Cao'
Talented strategist of the Lam Son uprising

name: Quang Trung
position: Emperor of the Tay Son Dynasty
title: The commoner hero
description: A military genius who conducted a rapid march to defeat 290,000 Qing troops during a few short Tet days.
achievements: Great victory at Ngoc Hoi - Dong Da
Defeated the Siamese army at Rach Gam - Xoai Mut

name: Truong Dinh
title: Binh Tay Dai Nguyen Soai
description: A resilient leader who chose to disobey the king's orders to stay with the people and fight the French to protect the Southern land.
achievements: Commanded the Go Cong insurgents against the French
Maintained indomitable heroic spirit

name: Ham Nghi
position: The 8th Emperor of the Nguyen Dynasty
title: The young patriotic king
description: A brave king who left the capital for the war zone, issued the Can Vuong Edict to ignite the flames of patriotism.
achievements: Issued the Can Vuong Edict to lead the resistance
Symbol of the royal court's anti-French spirit

name: Phan Boi Chau
title: Initiator of the Dong Du movement
description: A great patriot who advocated for people's education by sending young people to study in Japan to find a way to save the country.
achievements: Founded the Vietnam Quang Phuc Hoi
Initiated the Dong Du movement

name: Ho Chi Minh
realName: Nguyen Sinh Cung / Nguyen Tat Thanh
position: President of the Democratic Republic of Vietnam
title: Beloved Uncle Ho
description: A great leader of the nation, who found the right path to save the country and gave birth to the new Vietnam.
achievements: Read the Declaration of Independence on September 2, 1945
Led the resistance wars against France and the US

name: Vo Nguyen Giap
title: The people's General
description: A legendary general who commanded the Dien Bien Phu campaign, 'famous across five continents', one of the military geniuses of the 20th century.
achievements: Great victory at Dien Bien Phu in 1954
Commander-in-Chief of the Ho Chi Minh campaign in 1975
